title: Ayenia heteromorpha
taxonomy: Ayenia heteromorpha is classified as a species within the genus Ayenia, which is a part of the family Malpighiaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Barbados cherry family. The Malpighiaceae family is further classified under the order Malpighiales, which is a part of the clade Rosidae. The clade Rosidae is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help in treating various health issues. intro: Ayenia heteromorpha, also known as "Guayacanillo" or "Small Ayenia", is a small, shrub-like plant belonging to the Malpighiaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ayenia heteromorpha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ayenia heteromorpha include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to harness its therapeutic potential.
